title: API Integration & Axios Interceptors

questions.question_text: What is the main advantage of using an Axios Request Interceptor?
options: It makes the API call faster
It allows you to automatically attach headers (like Auth tokens) to every request
It replaces the need for a backend
It encrypts the entire request body
questions.explanation: Interceptors allow you to centralize common logic like authentication headers or logging for every outgoing request.

questions.question_text: What is the primary way a React frontend communicates with an Express backend?
options: Directly importing JS files from the server
questions.explanation: Modern fullstack apps use APIs to exchange data between the frontend and backend.
CORS is a security feature that controls which domains can access your API.

questions.question_text: Why do you usually need the `cors` package in Express?
options: To allow the React dev server (e.g., port 5173) to request data from the Express server (e.g., port 5000)
questions.explanation: By default, browsers block requests to a different port/domain for security.

options: A configuration that redirects API calls to the backend server so you don't have to write the full URL every time
questions.explanation: Proxying also helps avoid CORS issues during development.

questions.question_text: Which React Hook is most commonly used to fetch data on component load?
questions.explanation: useEffect triggers the fetch call when the component mounts.

options: A popular promise-based HTTP client for the browser and Node.js
questions.explanation: Axios simplifies data fetching compared to the native `fetch()` API.

questions.question_text: How do you handle 'Loading States' in the frontend?
options: Using a boolean state (e.g., isLoading) that shows a spinner while data is being fetched
questions.explanation: Good UX requires showing the user that work is happening.

questions.question_text: What is the benefit of keeping Your API base URL in an environment variable?
options: It makes it easier to switch between 'localhost' (dev) and 'https://api.myapp.com' (prod)
It hides the URL from hackers
questions.explanation: Hardcoding URLs makes deployment very difficult.

options: The process of converting a JavaScript object into a JSON string for transmission over the network
questions.explanation: The client serializes data to send, and the server deserializes it to read.

questions.question_text: Which HTTP method is used to send NEW data from a React form to the Express server?
questions.explanation: POST is used for creating new resources.

questions.question_text: How do you handle server errors (like 500) in React?
options: Using a try/catch block around the fetch call and setting an 'error' state for the UI
questions.explanation: You should show a friendly error message to the user.

questions.question_text: What is a 'Slug' in a URL?
options: A human-readable string used in the URL to identify a specific resource (e.g., /blog/how-to-code)
questions.explanation: Slugs are better for SEO and readability than numeric IDs.

questions.question_text: What is 'Pagination' in a Fullstack app?
options: Breaking data into pages so you don't load 10,000 items at once
questions.explanation: Essential for performance on lists with many items.

questions.question_text: What is the 'Network' tab in Chrome DevTools used for?
options: Inspecting API requests, viewing response bodies, and checking status codes
questions.explanation: It's the most important tool for debugging fullstack integration.

questions.question_text: True or False: The frontend should trust ALL data sent to the API is valid.
questions.explanation: Never trust the client. Always validate data on the backend (server-side) too.
